question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response caching on the GET /api/v1/network/stake endpoint to observe rapid changes in nodeRewardFeeFraction or stakingRewardFeeFraction between consecutive calls, inferring that fee parameters are being updated in real time and timing transactions to exploit the window between old and new fee values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the GET /api/v1/network/stake endpoint has no authentication requirement, allowing them to continuously poll findLatest() to detect the exact moment a NodeStakeUpdate transaction changes stakingRewardRate or nodeRewardFeeFraction, and front-run fee-sensitive transactions before other users are aware of the change?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network_stake table has no CHECK constraint ensuring epochDay is consistent with consensusTimestamp (epochDay = getEpochDay(consensusTimestamp) - 1), so that findLatest() could return a row with an epochDay that does not correspond to the consensusTimestamp, causing clients to apply fee parameters to the wrong staking epoch outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a CHECK constraint ensuring stakingPeriod < consensusTimestamp in the network_stake table, so that findLatest() returns a row where stakingPeriod is in the future relative to consensusTimestamp, causing clients to compute incorrect staking period ranges via mapTimestampRange(stakingPeriod) and apply wrong fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CrudRepository base interface exposes findById(Long id) on NetworkStakeRepository, allowing them to retrieve any historical network_stake row by guessing or brute-forcing consensusTimestamp values, and compare historical fee parameters (nodeRewardFeeFraction, stakingRewardRate) to detect unauthorized modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CrudRepository base interface exposes findAll() on NetworkStakeRepository (if not restricted by Spring Security), allowing them to retrieve the entire history of network_stake rows and reconstruct the full timeline of fee parameter changes (stakingRewardRate, nodeRewardFeeFraction) to identify anomalous modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CrudRepository base interface exposes count() on NetworkStakeRepository, allowing them to determine the total number of NodeStakeUpdate transactions processed, and use this to infer the frequency of fee parameter changes and detect anomalous update patterns outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CrudRepository base interface exposes existsById(Long id) on NetworkStakeRepository, allowing them to probe specific consensusTimestamp values to determine whether a NodeStakeUpdate occurred at a specific time, leaking information about fee parameter update timing outside design expectations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network_stake table stores nodeRewardFeeNumerator and nodeRewardFeeDenominator as separate long fields with no atomicity guarantee at the application layer, so that findLatest() could return a row where the numerator was updated by one transaction but the denominator reflects a previous period, producing a nodeRewardFeeFraction that is a hybrid of two different fee configurations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network_stake table stores stakingRewardFeeNumerator and stakingRewardFeeDenominator as separate long fields with no atomicity guarantee at the application layer, so that findLatest() could return a row where the numerator was updated by one transaction but the denominator reflects a previous period, producing a stakingRewardFeeFraction that is a hybrid of two different fee configurations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the GET /api/v1/network/stake endpoint returns nodeRewardFeeFraction as a float (32-bit), causing precision loss when nodeRewardFeeNumerator/nodeRewardFeeDenominator involves large values, so that the returned fraction differs from the actual on-chain value, causing clients to compute node fee allocations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the GET /api/v1/network/stake endpoint returns stakingRewardFeeFraction as a float (32-bit), causing precision loss when stakingRewardFeeNumerator/stakingRewardFeeDenominator involves large values, so that the returned fraction differs from the actual on-chain value, causing clients to compute staking reward fee allocations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network_stake table has no CHECK constraint ensuring nodeRewardFeeNumerator >= 0, so that findLatest() returns a row with a negative nodeRewardFeeNumerator, causing mapFraction() to return a negative nodeRewardFeeFraction, misrepresenting the node reward fee as a rebate rather than a deduction outside design parameters?
